name: PUA - Sysinternal Tool Execution - Registry
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Markus Neis
SIGMA Title: PUA - Sysinternal Tool Execution - Registry
SIGMA Description: Detects the execution of a Sysinternals Tool via the creation of the "accepteula" registry key

False positives:
- Legitimate use of SysInternals tools
- Programs that use the same Registry Key

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%createkey%' AND (LOWER("Process Name") LIKE '%\eulaaccepted' OR LOWER("Target Process Name") LIKE '%\eulaaccepted' OR LOWER("Object Name") LIKE '%\eulaaccepted') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY PUA - Sysinternal Tool Execution - Registry on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=12 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%createkey%' AND (LOWER("Process Name") LIKE '%\eulaaccepted' OR LOWER("Target Process Name") LIKE '%\eulaaccepted' OR LOWER("Object Name") LIKE '%\eulaaccepted') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: PUA - Sysinternal Tool Execution - Registry
